target.name: Labelbox
target.founder: Manu Sharma, Brian Rieger, Dan Rasmuson

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.3B - $183.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series G), post-money $183B

private.name: Together AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.3B

data.narrative:
Labelbox, a Series G software platform for AI & Software, has raised $188.9M to date against a last post-money valuation of $1.0B+. The relevant peer set comprises five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Anthropic ($183.0B, Series G), Waymo ($126.0B, Late Stage), Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), Dataminr ($4.1B, Series F), and Together AI ($3.3B, Series G). This universe spans nine private firms and one public comparable (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money and applying a 1.25x Series G step-up multiplier, yields $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across the five comps produces $15.0B, with a range of $3.3B to $183.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in business model and scale within frontier AI & Software: Anthropic's $183.0B valuation sits substantially above Labelbox's current position and capital raised, while Together AI at $3.3B provides a tighter peer floor.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $1.1B (low) to $17.2B (high), with a median of $8.1B. The gap between stage-progression ($1.2B) and peer-median ($15.0B) reflects uncertainty around whether Labelbox's market position and growth trajectory warrant valuation closer to later-stage frontier AI peers or closer to its prior fundraising round. Capital efficiency and competitive differentiation within data labeling and model training infrastructure remain critical variables.
